subject: Math

lesson.story_mission: Help the Data Detectives organize class survey results for the school fair.
lesson.mini_lesson: Graphs and tables organize counts so you can compare categories and answer questions.
lesson.objective: Represent and interpret data using picture graphs, bar graphs, line plots, and data tables.
lesson.concept_explanation: Picture graphs use symbols, bar graphs use bars, line plots use X marks, and data tables list counts. Use labels, keys, and scales before answering.
lesson.common_misconception: Learners may count one symbol as one even when the graph key uses a different scale, or compare the wrong categories.
lesson.real_world_connection: Students use graphs to summarize favorite snacks, books, sports, and measurements.
lesson.lesson_summary: Read the labels, use the key or scale, find the category count, and subtract to compare how many more or less.
hints: Read the title and labels first.
Use the key or scale before counting.
For more or less, subtract the smaller count from the larger count.

worked_examples.text: If a picture graph key says ⭐ = 2 votes, then 4 stars show 8 votes.
If apples have 9 votes and grapes have 5 votes, apples have 4 more votes because 9 - 5 = 4.
On a line plot, each X over a number means one measurement with that value.

hint_ladder: Read the title and labels first.
Use the key or scale before counting.
For more or less, subtract the smaller count from the larger count.

misconception_bank.graph_scale_confusion: Learner may ignore the graph key or bar scale.
misconception_bank.category_count_error: Learner may count the wrong category.
misconception_bank.more_less_data_confusion: Learner may add instead of subtract for more/less questions.
misconception_bank.line_plot_mark_error: Learner may place or count X marks at the wrong number.

game_theme.title: Data, Graphs, and Line Plots
game_theme.mission_intro: Help the Data Detectives organize class survey results for the school fair.

guided_practice.prompt: Study the picture graph. How many are recorded for Tulips? Use the display titled Garden Votes.
hints: Focus: Find the Tulips label in Garden Votes.
Strategy: Read the value aligned with the requested label.
Verify: Check the Tulips, Daisies, Lilies values against the question before submitting on Garden Votes.

feedback.incorrect: Check the labels and marks, then try again.

guided_practice.explanation: The Tulips row shows 4. Reading that labeled row gives 4.

question_audio.text: Study the picture graph. How many are recorded for Tulips? Use the display titled Garden Votes. A picture graph titled Garden Votes with three labeled categories: Tulips, Daisies, Lilies. The key says one star stands for 1; symbols are arranged in labeled rows.
question_audio.label: Read Question

guided_practice.visual_description: A picture graph titled Garden Votes with three labeled categories: Tulips, Daisies, Lilies. The key says one star stands for 1; symbols are arranged in labeled rows.
guided_practice.accessible_description: A picture graph titled Garden Votes with three labeled categories: Tulips, Daisies, Lilies. The key says one star stands for 1; symbols are arranged in labeled rows.

guided_practice.prompt: How many more Turtles than Birds are shown in the picture graph? Use the display titled Pet Survey.
hints: Focus: Find the Turtles label in Pet Survey.
Strategy: Compare 10 and 6 by subtracting the smaller count.
Verify: Check the Rabbits, Turtles, Birds values against the question before submitting on Pet Survey.

feedback.incorrect: Check the labels and marks, then try again.

guided_practice.explanation: The display shows 10 for Turtles and 6 for Birds. Subtracting 10 − 6 gives 4 more.

question_audio.text: How many more Turtles than Birds are shown in the picture graph? Use the display titled Pet Survey. A picture graph titled Pet Survey with three labeled categories: Rabbits, Turtles, Birds. The key says one star stands for 2; symbols are arranged in labeled rows.
question_audio.label: Read Question

guided_practice.visual_description: A picture graph titled Pet Survey with three labeled categories: Rabbits, Turtles, Birds. The key says one star stands for 2; symbols are arranged in labeled rows.
guided_practice.accessible_description: A picture graph titled Pet Survey with three labeled categories: Rabbits, Turtles, Birds. The key says one star stands for 2; symbols are arranged in labeled rows.

guided_practice.prompt: How many are shown for Chalk and Hoops altogether? Use the display titled Recess Choices.
hints: Focus: Find the Chalk label in Recess Choices.
Strategy: Combine only the Chalk and Hoops counts.
Verify: Check the Chalk, Bubbles, Hoops values against the question before submitting on Recess Choices.

feedback.incorrect: Check the labels and marks, then try again.

guided_practice.explanation: The two labeled counts are 6 and 5. Adding them, 6 + 5, gives 11 altogether.

question_audio.text: How many are shown for Chalk and Hoops altogether? Use the display titled Recess Choices. A picture graph titled Recess Choices with three labeled categories: Chalk, Bubbles, Hoops. The key says one star stands for 1; symbols are arranged in labeled rows.
question_audio.label: Read Question

guided_practice.visual_description: A picture graph titled Recess Choices with three labeled categories: Chalk, Bubbles, Hoops. The key says one star stands for 1; symbols are arranged in labeled rows.
guided_practice.accessible_description: A picture graph titled Recess Choices with three labeled categories: Chalk, Bubbles, Hoops. The key says one star stands for 1; symbols are arranged in labeled rows.

hints: Focus: Find the Robins label in Trail Birds.
Strategy: Compare 8 and 4 by subtracting the smaller count.
Verify: Check the Wrens, Robins, Finches values against the question before submitting on Trail Birds.

feedback.incorrect: Check the labels and marks, then try again.

adaptive_question_bank.explanation: The display shows 8 for Robins and 4 for Finches. Subtracting 8 − 4 gives 4 more.

adaptive_question_bank.prompt: On the Button Widths line plot, how many measurements are at 4?
hints: Focus: Look at the X stacks on Button Widths, not empty number labels.
Strategy: Count the stack connected to the number named in the question.
Verify: Match each counted X to one item in the measurement list on Button Widths.

feedback.incorrect: Check the labels and marks, then try again.

adaptive_question_bank.explanation: There are 3 X marks stacked over 4. Each X is one measurement, so the count is 3.

question_audio.text: On the Button Widths line plot, how many measurements are at 4? A line plot titled Button Widths, labeled from 2 through 6. X marks are stacked above the number for each measurement.
question_audio.label: Read Question

adaptive_question_bank.visual_description: A line plot titled Button Widths, labeled from 2 through 6. X marks are stacked above the number for each measurement.
adaptive_question_bank.accessible_description: A line plot titled Button Widths, labeled from 2 through 6. X marks are stacked above the number for each measurement.

adaptive_question_bank.prompt: How many measurements are shown altogether on the Crayon Lengths line plot?
hints: Focus: Look at the X stacks on Crayon Lengths, not empty number labels.
Strategy: Count every X once across all stacks.
Verify: Match each counted X to one item in the measurement list on Crayon Lengths.

feedback.incorrect: Check the labels and marks, then try again.

adaptive_question_bank.explanation: Each X represents one measurement. Counting all 8 X marks gives 8 measurements altogether.

question_audio.text: How many measurements are shown altogether on the Crayon Lengths line plot? A line plot titled Crayon Lengths, labeled from 3 through 8. X marks are stacked above the number for each measurement.
question_audio.label: Read Question

adaptive_question_bank.accessible_description: A line plot titled Crayon Lengths, labeled from 3 through 8. X marks are stacked above the number for each measurement.

questions.prompt: The key says each star stands for 2. How many stars represent the 10 Dance responses? Use the display titled Club Sign-ups.

level_banks.label: Level 4: Line Plots
level_banks.focus: Interpret X marks on line plots.

questions.prompt: On the Button Widths line plot, how many measurements are at 4?
hints: Focus: Look at the X stacks on Button Widths, not empty number labels.
Strategy: Count the stack connected to the number named in the question.
Verify: Match each counted X to one item in the measurement list on Button Widths.

feedback.incorrect: Check the labels and marks, then try again.

questions.explanation: There are 3 X marks stacked over 4. Each X is one measurement, so the count is 3.

question_audio.text: On the Button Widths line plot, how many measurements are at 4? A line plot titled Button Widths, labeled from 2 through 6. X marks are stacked above the number for each measurement.
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.visual_description: A line plot titled Button Widths, labeled from 2 through 6. X marks are stacked above the number for each measurement.
questions.accessible_description: A line plot titled Button Widths, labeled from 2 through 6. X marks are stacked above the number for each measurement.

questions.prompt: How many measurements are shown altogether on the Crayon Lengths line plot?
hints: Focus: Look at the X stacks on Crayon Lengths, not empty number labels.
Strategy: Count every X once across all stacks.
Verify: Match each counted X to one item in the measurement list on Crayon Lengths.

feedback.incorrect: Check the labels and marks, then try again.

questions.explanation: Each X represents one measurement. Counting all 8 X marks gives 8 measurements altogether.

question_audio.text: How many measurements are shown altogether on the Crayon Lengths line plot? A line plot titled Crayon Lengths, labeled from 3 through 8. X marks are stacked above the number for each measurement.

questions.prompt: Which measurement occurs most often on the Bean Sprout Heights line plot?
hints: Focus: Look at the X stacks on Bean Sprout Heights, not empty number labels.
Strategy: Count the stack connected to the number named in the question.
Verify: Match each counted X to one item in the measurement list on Bean Sprout Heights.

feedback.incorrect: Check the labels and marks, then try again.

questions.explanation: The tallest stack has 3 X marks and sits over 6. Therefore 6 occurs most often.

Verify: Explain why your chosen method fits the line plot before submitting on Shell Lengths.

feedback.incorrect: Check the labels and marks, then try again.

questions.explanation: There are 3 marks at 15 and 1 at 13. The difference 3 − 1 is 2.

question_audio.text: Choose the useful graph-reading method. How many more measurements are at 15 than at 13? Use the display titled Shell Lengths. A line plot titled Shell Lengths, labeled from 13 through 18. X marks are stacked above the number for each measurement.
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.visual_description: A line plot titled Shell Lengths, labeled from 13 through 18. X marks are stacked above the number for each measurement.
questions.accessible_description: A line plot titled Shell Lengths, labeled from 13 through 18. X marks are stacked above the number for each measurement.

questions.prompt: Choose the useful graph-reading method. The key says each star stands for 2. How many stars represent the 26 Space responses? Use the display titled Museum Favorites.
hints: Focus: Decide whether this display calls for counting marks, reading a labeled value, comparing, or combining.
Strategy: Use groups of 2, not single votes.
Verify: Explain why your chosen method fits the picture graph before submitting on Museum Favorites.

feedback.incorrect: Check the labels and marks, then try again.

questions.explanation: Space has 26 responses. Making groups of 2, 26 ÷ 2 = 13, so that many stars are needed.

question_audio.text: Choose the useful graph-reading method. The key says each star stands for 2. How many stars represent the 26 Space responses? Use the display titled Museum Favorites. A picture graph titled Museum Favorites with three labeled categories: Dinosaurs, Space, Trains. The key says one star stands for 2; symbols are arranged in labeled rows.
question_audio.label: Read Question

questions.visual_description: A picture graph titled Museum Favorites with three labeled categories: Dinosaurs, Space, Trains. The key says one star stands for 2; symbols are arranged in labeled rows.
questions.accessible_description: A picture graph titled Museum Favorites with three labeled categories: Dinosaurs, Space, Trains. The key says one star stands for 2; symbols are arranged in labeled rows.

questions.prompt: Choose the useful graph-reading method. Study the bar graph. How many are recorded for Oak Stop? Use the display titled Bus Riders.
hints: Focus: Decide whether this display calls for counting marks, reading a labeled value, comparing, or combining.
Strategy: Read the value aligned with the requested label.
Verify: Explain why your chosen method fits the bar graph before submitting on Bus Riders.

feedback.incorrect: Check the labels and marks, then try again.

questions.explanation: The Oak Stop row shows 19. Reading that labeled row gives 19.

question_audio.text: Choose the useful graph-reading method. Study the bar graph. How many are recorded for Oak Stop? Use the display titled Bus Riders. questions.prompt: Choose the useful graph-reading method. How many more Bagels than Rolls are shown in the table? Use the display titled Bake Sale.
hints: Focus: Decide whether this display calls for counting marks, reading a labeled value, comparing, or combining.
Strategy: Compare 28 and 16 by subtracting the smaller count.
Verify: Explain why your chosen method fits the data table before submitting on Bake Sale.

feedback.incorrect: Check the labels and marks, then try again.

questions.explanation: The display shows 28 for Bagels and 16 for Rolls. Subtracting 28 − 16 gives 12 more.

question_audio.text: Choose the useful graph-reading method. How many more Bagels than Rolls are shown in the table? Use the display titled Bake Sale. A data table titled Bake Sale with three labeled categories: Muffins, Bagels, Rolls. Each category is in its own row beside its count.

questions.prompt: Choose the useful graph-reading method. Which measurement occurs most often on the Ribbon Lengths line plot?
hints: Focus: Decide whether this display calls for counting marks, reading a labeled value, comparing, or combining.
Strategy: Count the stack connected to the number named in the question.
Verify: Explain why your chosen method fits the line plot before submitting on Ribbon Lengths.

feedback.incorrect: Check the labels and marks, then try again.

questions.explanation: The tallest stack has 3 X marks and sits over 19. Therefore 19 occurs most often.

questions.explanation: Comparing 22, 30, 18 shows that 30 is greatest, so its category, Dance, has the greatest count.

questions.prompt: Choose the useful graph-reading method. What is the greatest measurement that has an X on the Pencil Lengths line plot?
hints: Focus: Decide whether this display calls for counting marks, reading a labeled value, comparing, or combining.
Strategy: Move from right to left until you find a number with an X.
Verify: Explain why your chosen method fits the line plot before submitting on Pencil Lengths.

feedback.incorrect: Check the labels and marks, then try again.

questions.explanation: Scan rightward only to labels that have an X. The rightmost marked value is 24, so it is the greatest measurement recorded.

lesson.text: This lesson teaches Data, Graphs, and Line Plots. Look at the picture graph and connect each part to the math words. Use the vocabulary on the page to explain your thinking. Remember to check place value, units, or symbols before you answer. Press Next to continue.

watch.text: This page lets you watch a worked example for Data, Graphs, and Line Plots. Follow the model one step at a time. Say what each number means before you look at the final answer.
